title: trifolium andricum
intro: Trifolium andricum, also known as the Andrican clover, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, specifically a type of perennial legume found in the Mediterranean region. This plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Trifolium andricum include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit immunomodulatory activities, making it a valuable plant for traditional medicine. Trifolium andricum is traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
